quotes.text: People think I sit down and the whole thing just pours out. But some of my best songs took two years of avoiding them before I could finish them. Distance is an instrument too.
quotes.speaker: Taylor Swift
quotes.context: Interview with Rolling Stone during the Midnights promo cycle
quotes.fake_note: Taylor frequently discusses her songwriting process and uses literary metaphors, so 'distance is an instrument' sounds exactly like her articulate, introspective voice. The subtle giveaway is that she more often emphasizes writing quickly and prolifically — she rarely frames avoidance as a method.

quotes.text: Jazz taught me that the note you don't play is doing half the work. I try to bring that into everything — production, performance, even conversations.
quotes.speaker: Kendrick Lamar
quotes.context: Rare print interview discussing musical influences
quotes.fake_note: Kendrick has cited jazz as a core influence and speaks about music with conceptual depth; the extension of the idea into 'even conversations' fits his introspective public persona. quotes.text: There was a period where I wouldn't play a show unless the monitors were set up the exact same way every single night. I've loosened up on some things. Not that one.

quotes.text: People want the gospel of hip-hop delivered like a TED Talk. But the whole point is that it was never supposed to be comfortable for everyone in the room.
quotes.speaker: Questlove
quotes.context: Panel discussion at SXSW on hip-hop's cultural legacy
quotes.fake_note: Questlove is an articulate cultural commentator who regularly speaks on hip-hop's intellectual credibility, and the TED Talk reference fits his world perfectly. quotes.speaker: Dolly Parton
